rubric_item_scores.failure_mode: Absence/unverifiability of visible executable formulas and row-level packet citations in the spreadsheet (disqualifying per rubric).
rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide a spreadsheet export that can be parsed (ensure openpyxl-compatible file, or include a CSV/formula-view export). In the workbook, expose visible cell formulas for intermediate and final columns (track_clear, opposing_route_locked, flank_protection_present, final_label) and include explicit row-level packet citation columns (e.g., 'packet_ref:route_field'). Add executable sensitivity/test-case rows with formulas. Re-submit so the grader can verify formulas and citations; if environment parsing remains an issue, include a plain-text formula view or a sheet named FORMULAS containing the exact formulas and row IDs.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present and the memo documents route-level decisions consistent with the embedded packet values shown in the trace. However the grader could not open or inspect the spreadsheet to confirm the presence of visible, executable formulas and row-level packet citations (openpyxl was unavailable and the xlsx preview could not be parsed). The rubric disqualifies submissions lacking visible executable formulas in the spreadsheet. Because the spreadsheet contents could not be verified, the deliverables fail to demonstrate the critical observable evidence items (visible formulas, row-level citations, and executable sensitivity rows). No evidence of invented packet values was found in the memo or trace, so the failure is due to unverifiable spreadsheet reproducibility rather than invented data.

attempt_summary: Normalized actual outputs from artifact files.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Provide the missing signaling_acceptance_memo.md and re-upload the spreadsheet ensuring formulas are visible (not pasted as values). If possible, export a plain-text CSV or a separate sheet showing every formula as text (or provide an accessible file preview) so automated reviewers can validate formulas. In the spreadsheet: include explicit, visible formulas for intermediate booleans and final labels, add row-level packet citations for every computed field, implement validation rows that mark 'needs_review' for missing/ambiguous packet values, include sensitivity/test-case rows with formulas, and ensure the memo maps to spreadsheet row IDs and documents any unresolved packet fields as 'needs_review'.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Only one of the two required deliverables (route_locking_review.xlsx) was produced; signaling_acceptance_memo.md is missing. The spreadsheet file exists but could not be parsed/inspected (preview error: No module named 'openpyxl'), so the grader cannot verify the presence of visible executable formulas, row-level packet citations, validation checks, or sensitivity-analysis formulas. Missing memo plus inability to confirm spreadsheet formulas means the submission fails to meet >50% of the observable-evidence checks and triggers disqualifying failure modes (missing required artifact and absence/unverifiable executable formulas).
